title: A queen's tale: Assessing the hidden potential of beeswax specimens in Natural History Museum collections
author: Kasso, Tuuli
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study investigated a 19th-century queen bee cell from a natural history museum to reveal a perfectly preserved queen bee and successfully extracted proteins from the inside of the cell with information about the development and diet of the bee. These findings offer valuable insights into historical bee rearing practices, which can help us better understand and address current challenges faced by honeybees.
steps_raw: This study investigated a 19th-century queen bee cell from a natural history museum to reveal a perfectly preserved queen bee and successfully extracted proteins from the inside of the cell with information about the development and diet of the bee. These findings offer valuable insights into historical bee rearing practices, which can help us better understand and address current challenges faced by honeybees.
